papers.title_en: Towards grounded autonomous research: an end-to-end LLM mini research loop on published computational physics
papers.evidence: 用于计算物理自主研究的大模型智能体
papers.tldr: 本研究探讨了LLM智能体在计算物理领域的自主研究能力。研究者构建了一个包含阅读、复现、批判和扩展的“微型研究循环”，并在111篇开源论文的大规模测试和单篇高质量论文的深度分析中进行了验证。结果显示，智能体能自主发现约42%论文中的实质性问题，并能针对特定研究生成具有出版质量的评论文章，甚至修正原论文的核心结论，展示了AI在辅助科学发现和同行评审中的巨大潜力。
papers.abstract_en: Recent autonomous LLM agents have demonstrated end-to-end automation of machine-learning research. Real-world physical science is intrinsically harder, requiring deep reasoning bounded by physical truth and, because real systems are too complex to study in isolation, almost always built on existing literature. We focus on the smallest meaningful unit of such research, a mini research loop in which an agent reads a paper, reproduces it, critiques it, and extends it. We test this loop in two complementary regimes: scale and depth. At scale, across 111 open-access computational physics papers, an agent autonomously runs the read-plan-compute-compare loop and, without being asked to critique, raises substantive concerns on ~42% of papers - 97.7% of which require execution to surface. In depth, for one Nature Communications paper on multiscale simulation of a 2D-material MOSFET, the agent runs new calculations missing from the original and produces, unsupervised, a publishable Comment -- composed, figured, typeset, and PDF-iterated -- that revises the paper's headline conclusion.

papers.title_en: The Agentification of Scientific Research: A Physicist's Perspective
papers.evidence: 物理学家关于AI智能体用于科学发现的视角
papers.tldr: 本文从物理学家视角探讨了AI（尤其是大语言模型）对科学研究的深远影响。作者认为AI革命的核心在于改变了复杂信息和人类诀窍的承载与传播方式。文章勾勒了AI从辅助工具演变为科学协作伙伴的路径，预示了其对科研协作、发现、出版及评估体系的根本性重塑，并强调了持续学习与思想多样性在实现AI驱动的原创性科学发现中的核心地位。
papers.abstract_en: This article argues that the most important significance of the AI revolution, especially the rise of large language models, lies not simply in automation, but in a fundamental change in how complex information and human know-how are carried, replicated, and shared. From this perspective, AI for Science is especially important because it may transform not only the efficiency of research, but also the structure of scientific collaboration, discovery, publishing, and evaluation. The article outlines a gradual path from AI as a research tool to AI as a scientific collaborator, and discusses how AI is likely to fundamentally reshape scientific publication. It also argues that continuous learning and diversity of ideas are essential if AI is to play a meaningful role in original scientific discovery.

papers.title_en: Vector Field Synthesis with Sparse Streamlines Using Diffusion Model
papers.authors: Nguyen K. Phan, Ricardo Morales, Sebastian D. Espriella, Guoning Chen
papers.evidence: 具有物理约束的矢量场合成扩散模型
papers.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于扩散模型的二维向量场合成框架，旨在从稀疏流线输入中重建具有物理合理性的向量场。该方法结合条件去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）与无分类器指导技术，实现了在保留几何约束的同时确保物理一致性。研究解决了传统优化方法在处理稀疏观测数据时灵活性不足的问题，为物理感知的数据合成提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: We present a novel diffusion-based framework for synthesizing 2D vector fields from sparse, coherent inputs (i.e., streamlines) while maintaining physical plausibility. Our method employs a conditional denoising diffusion probabilistic model with classifier-free guidance, enabling progressive reconstruction that preserves both geometric and physical constraints. Experimental results demonstrate our method's ability to synthesize plausible vector fields that adhere to physical laws while maintaining fidelity to sparse input observations, outperforming traditional optimization-based approaches in terms of flexibility and physical consistency.

papers.title_en: Structure- and Stability-Preserving Learning of Port-Hamiltonian Systems
papers.authors: Binh Nguyen, Nam T. Nguyen, Truong X. Nghiem
papers.evidence: 基于神经网络的哈密顿系统建模
papers.tldr: 本文提出一种新型神经网络方法，用于端口哈密顿系统的结构与稳定性保持建模。该方法通过放宽哈密顿函数的凸性约束，增强了模型的表达能力与泛化性，并实现了对多个孤立稳定平衡点的学习与保持。实验证明，该方法在处理复杂动力学系统时，比传统基准方法具有更高的建模精度和灵活性，有效解决了结构保持与模型表达力之间的平衡问题。
papers.abstract_en: This paper investigates the problem of data-driven modeling of port-Hamiltonian systems while preserving their intrinsic Hamiltonian structure and stability properties. We propose a novel neural-network-based port-Hamiltonian modeling technique that relaxes the convexity constraint commonly imposed by neural network-based Hamiltonian approximations, thereby improving the expressiveness and generalization capability of the model. By removing this restriction, the proposed approach enables the use of more general non-convex Hamiltonian representations to enhance modeling flexibility and accuracy. Furthermore, the proposed method incorporates information about stable equilibria into the learning process, allowing the learned model to preserve the stability of multiple isolated equilibria rather than being restricted to a single equilibrium as in conventional methods. Two numerical experiments are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach and demonstrate its ability to achieve more accurate structure- and stability-preserving learning of port-Hamiltonian systems compared with a baseline method.

papers.title_en: MARS$^2$: Scaling Multi-Agent Tree Search via Reinforcement Learning for Code Generation
papers.authors: Pengfei Li, Shijie Wang, Fangyuan Li, Yikun Fu, Kaifeng Liu, Kaiyan Zhang, Dazhi Zhang, Yuqiang Li, Biqing Qi, Bowen Zhou
papers.evidence: 多智能体强化学习用于代码和算法发现
papers.tldr: 本文针对代码生成任务中强化学习轨迹多样性不足的问题，提出了MARS$^2$框架。该框架将多智能体协作与树搜索深度融合，允许异构智能体在共享的树结构环境中协同探索与优化。通过引入路径级组优势和树一致性奖励塑造，解决了复杂搜索中的信用分配难题，有效提升了模型的推理与生成能力。
papers.abstract_en: Reinforcement learning (RL) paradigms have demonstrated strong performance on reasoning-intensive tasks such as code generation. However, limited trajectory diversity often leads to diminishing returns, which constrains the achievable performance ceiling. Search-enhanced RL alleviates this issue by introducing structured exploration, which remains constrained by the single-agent policy priors. Meanwhile, leveraging multiple interacting policies can acquire more diverse exploratory signals, but existing approaches are typically decoupled from structured search. We propose \textbf{MARS$^2$} (Multi-Agent Reinforced Tree-Search Scaling), a unified RL framework in which multiple independently-optimized agents collaborate within a shared tree-structured search environment. MARS$^2$ models the search tree as a learnable multi-agent interaction environment, enabling heterogeneous agents to collaboratively generate and refine candidate solutions within a shared search topology. To support effective learning, we introduce a path-level group advantage formulation based on tree-consistent reward shaping, which facilitates effective credit assignment across complex search trajectories. Experiments on code generation benchmarks show that MARS$^2$ consistently improves performance across diverse model combinations and training settings, demonstrating the effectiveness of coupling multi-agent collaboration with tree search for enhancing reinforcement learning. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/TsinghuaC3I/MARTI.
